nodes.name: Local File Trigger
nodes.notes: Production V1 keeps this workflow inactive and uses manual n8n runs. Only activate this trigger after validating raw/** watcher behavior.

nodes.name: Acquire Bot Lock
nodes.notes: Tries to acquire the shared bot lock. If another ingest (e.g. triggered by the answer workflow) is already running, the lock will not be acquired and execution stops — preventing duplicate parallel ingests.

nodes.name: Parse File Watcher Lock
nodes.notes: If lock was not acquired (another ingest is active), returns [] to silently stop this execution. Otherwise passes the lock_id downstream so it can be released at the end.

nodes.name: Run ingest-run.ts
nodes.notes: Runs the full deterministic ingest macro: source normalization, LLM cleanup/planning, guarded wiki updates, reindex, and commit.

nodes.name: Send Telegram Ingest Log
nodes.notes: Sends a plain-text Telegram message through Bot API. Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and a chat id.

parameters.command: ={{ $json.telegram_lock_id ? "node /app/dist/tools/cmd/bot-lock.js release --vault /data/vault --name telegram-bot --lock-id " + JSON.stringify($json.telegram_lock_id) : "node -e " + JSON.stringify("process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ status: 'skipped', released: false }) + '\n')") }}

nodes.name: Release Bot Lock After Ingest
nodes.notes: Releases the bot lock acquired at the start of this workflow, allowing subsequent ingests to proceed.
